findings.codeql_raw_query:
/**
 * @name Find all command execution sites in git client plugin
 * @description Locate all places where OS commands are executed
 * @kind problem
 * @problem.severity warning
 * @id java/git-command-execution-sites
 * @tags security
 *       external/cwe/cwe-078
 */

import java

from MethodCall mc
where
  (
    mc.getMethod().hasName("exec") and
    mc.getMethod().getDeclaringType().hasQualifiedName("java.lang", "Runtime")
  ) or (
    mc.getMethod().hasName("command") and
    mc.getMethod().getDeclaringType().hasQualifiedName("java.lang", "ProcessBuilder")
  ) or (
    mc.getMethod().hasName("launch") and
    mc.getMethod().getDeclaringType().getASupertype*().hasQualifiedName("hudson", "Launcher")
  ) or (
    mc.getMethod().hasName("launchChannel") and
    mc.getMethod().getDeclaringType().getASupertype*().hasQualifiedName("hudson", "Launcher")
  )
select mc, "Command execution site: " + mc.getMethod().getName() + " in " + mc.getEnclosingCallable().getName()
findings.hypothesis: Identify all command execution sites in the codebase

message.text: Command execution site: launch in execute
Command execution site: launch in launchCommandIn

findings.codeql_raw_query:
/**
 * @name Identify source methods and parameters for git argument injection
 * @description Find the exact public methods and parameter names that flow to git command arguments
 * @kind problem
 * @problem.severity error
 * @id java/git-client-source-methods
 * @tags security
 *       external/cwe/cwe-078
 *       external/cwe/cwe-088
 */

import java
import semmle.code.java.dataflow.TaintTracking

module GitArgInjectionConfig implements DataFlow::ConfigSig {
  predicate isSource(DataFlow::Node source) {
    exists(Parameter p |
      p.getCallable().getDeclaringType().hasQualifiedName("org.jenkinsci.plugins.gitclient", "CliGitAPIImpl") and
      p.getCallable().(Method).isPublic() and
      (p.getType() instanceof TypeString or p.getType().hasName("URIish")) and
      source.asParameter() = p
    )
  }

  predicate isSink(DataFlow::Node sink) {
    exists(MethodCall mc |
      mc.getEnclosingCallable().getDeclaringType().hasQualifiedName("org.jenkinsci.plugins.gitclient", "CliGitAPIImpl") and
      mc.getMethod().hasName("add") and
      mc.getMethod().getDeclaringType().hasQualifiedName("hudson.util", "ArgumentListBuilder") and
      sink.asExpr() = mc.getAnArgument()
    )
    or
    exists(MethodCall mc |
      mc.getMethod().hasName("launchCommandIn") and
      mc.getMethod().getDeclaringType().hasQualifiedName("org.jenkinsci.plugins.gitclient", "CliGitAPIImpl") and
      sink.asExpr() = mc.getAnArgument()
    )
  }
}

module GitArgInjectionFlow = TaintTracking::Global<GitArgInjectionConfig>;

// Show source parameter details
from DataFlow::Node source, DataFlow::Node sink, Parameter p
where 
  GitArgInjectionFlow::flow(source, sink) and
  source.asParameter() = p
select source, "Source: method '" + p.getCallable().getName() + "', param '" + p.getName() + "' (type: " + p.getType().getName() + ") -> sink at line " + sink.asExpr().getLocation().getStartLine()
findings.hypothesis: Identify the specific public methods and parameters that are source of argument injection vulnerabilities

findings.codeql_raw_query:
/**
 * @name Branch/refspec name injection in git commands - argument flag injection
 * @description User-controlled branch names or refspecs that start with '--' could inject flags into git commands
 * @kind problem
 * @problem.severity error
 * @id java/git-client-branch-name-injection
 * @tags security
 *       external/cwe/cwe-078
 *       external/cwe/cwe-088
 */

import java
import semmle.code.java.dataflow.TaintTracking

// Find all methods in CliGitAPIImpl that accept branch names, ref names, or remote names
// and add them directly to git command arguments without validation
from MethodCall addCall, Method enclosingMethod
where
  addCall.getMethod().hasName("add") and
  addCall.getMethod().getDeclaringType().hasQualifiedName("hudson.util", "ArgumentListBuilder") and
  addCall.getEnclosingCallable() = enclosingMethod and
  enclosingMethod.getDeclaringType().hasQualifiedName("org.jenkinsci.plugins.gitclient", "CliGitAPIImpl") and
  // The argument being added is not a string literal (not a fixed flag)
  not addCall.getArgument(0) instanceof StringLiteral and
  not addCall.getArgument(0) instanceof BooleanLiteral and
  // Filter out calls where argument comes directly from field access that is a fixed value
  not exists(FieldAccess fa | fa = addCall.getArgument(0))
select addCall, "Non-literal argument added to git command in method '" + enclosingMethod.getName() + "': " + addCall.getArgument(0).toString()
findings.hypothesis: Non-literal arguments added to git commands could enable flag injection if they start with '--'

findings.codeql_raw_query:
/**
 * @name Dangerous git environment variables set from user input
 * @description User input flows to git-interpreted environment variables like GIT_SSH, GIT_PROXY_COMMAND
 * @kind problem
 * @problem.severity error
 * @id java/git-dangerous-env-var
 * @tags security
 *       external/cwe/cwe-078
 */

import java
import semmle.code.java.dataflow.TaintTracking

// Find the specific env var keys being set from user input
from MethodCall mc, StringLiteral envVarName
where
  mc.getMethod().hasName("put") and
  mc.getEnclosingCallable().getDeclaringType().hasQualifiedName("org.jenkinsci.plugins.gitclient", "CliGitAPIImpl") and
  mc.getArgument(0) = envVarName and
  (
    envVarName.getValue().matches("GIT_SSH%") or
    envVarName.getValue().matches("GIT_PROXY%") or
    envVarName.getValue() = "GIT_ASKPASS" or
    envVarName.getValue() = "GIT_EXEC_PATH" or
    envVarName.getValue() = "GIT_AUTHOR_NAME" or
    envVarName.getValue() = "GIT_AUTHOR_EMAIL" or
    envVarName.getValue() = "GIT_COMMITTER_NAME" or
    envVarName.getValue() = "GIT_COMMITTER_EMAIL" or
    envVarName.getValue() = "GIT_CONFIG" or
    envVarName.getValue().matches("GIT_%")
  )
select mc, "Setting git environment variable '" + envVarName.getValue() + "' in method '" + mc.getEnclosingCallable().getName() + "' at line " + mc.getLocation().getStartLine()
findings.hypothesis: Dangerous git environment variables like GIT_SSH_COMMAND, GIT_PROXY_COMMAND being set from user input

message.text: Setting git environment variable 'GIT_TERMINAL_PROMPT' in method 'launchCommandWithCredentials' at line 1710
Setting git environment variable 'GIT_SSH' in method 'launchCommandWithCredentials' at line 1736

Setting git environment variable 'GIT_ASKPASS' in method 'launchCommandWithCredentials' at line 1757
